mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent above-target inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%, or inflation-adjusted earnings growth outperforms bond yields.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to generate positive 60d returns in > 70% of cases, or the correlation between the proxies and SPX drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4%) reduces wage pressures and corporate cost inflation, improving profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE remains below 4%, or wage growth outpaces earnings growth by > 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger manufacturing activity, increasing corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year window, or 20d returns decline despite rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) signals weakening demand, reducing corporate revenue expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances with declining RSAFS, or consumer spending declines correlate positively with equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A dovish monetary policy pivot (FEDFUNDS decline + CPIAUCSL rise) creates a 'stagflation' environment, increasing volatility and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling rates and rising inflation fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 70% of cases, or equity volatility declines despite the macro environment.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 200k) indicates labor market strength, increasing consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances with PAYEMS above 200k, or employment growth fails to correlate with equity returns (Rank IC < 0.10).
